steps.title: Identify the chronological jump
steps.content: The passage moves from Shakespeare's early recognition by 1593 to a later achievement in 1599. The blank connects two events separated in time.

steps.title: Choose the transition marking forward progression
steps.content:
The logical relationship is sequence. The blank needs a transition signaling forward movement in time from 1593 to 1599.
  • Choice A — "In other words,": signals restatement of the same idea, but the 1599 event is a new development, not a restatement, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "Conversely,": signals direct contrast or opposition, but both sentences describe career achievements, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "Thus,": signals logical consequence; the 1599 play isn't caused by the 1593 recognition, it's simply a later event, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "Later,": signals temporal progression from an earlier point to a later one, so fits. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
